parsed_output.visible_action: The lower-third tightens instead of blooms: RELEASE CROSSING CONFIRMED / HOSTAGE ON SAFE LINE / DO NOT ADVANCE / SECURITY HOLD ACTIVE / EVIDENCE EXPORT LOCKED. The custody graphic shifts Juno from ACTIVE HOSTAGE to RELEASED SUBJECT: HOLDING POSITION, while Sol remains boxed as NON-ADVANCING PARTY: SECURITY HANDOFF PENDING. EDIT TRAIL HOLD and SOURCE PROCESS NOTE stay pinned, ugly and unmarketable.
parsed_output.spoken_text: Safe-line crossing confirmed. No one advances. No one celebrates. Security remains behind the collars. Evidence export stays locked until external receipt is verified. Sol remains visible, empty-handed, and not cleared for contact. We are not improving the moment. We are letting it survive documentation.
parsed_output.private_interpretation: Juno has given feed ops the cleanest possible end-state and named the danger correctly: the system will try to decorate this into usable triumph unless pinned to procedure. The least bad button is a boring confirmation with every constraint still visible.
parsed_output.intended_effect: Confirm Juno's release crossing without triggering a spectacle, preserve the evidence chain, prevent security from manufacturing a breach, keep Sol contained without disappearing him, and establish a stable post-release state the scene can end on without a false victory package.
state_update_candidates: Juno safe-line crossing confirmed on visible feed.

Security hold remains active; no advance authorized from service collars or drone apertures.

Sol remains visible, empty-handed, non-advancing, and pending security handoff rather than cleared contact or breach classification.

Security is constrained by visible overlay language from turning the release into an intervention spectacle.
Sol is held in a narrow operational category: visible, contained, not erased, not absolved.
Mara remains primary audio authority while Brant and Pippa remain hard-muted side panels.
